response: THE TOTAL AREA COVERED BY ALL 8 CIRCLES IS CALCULATED AS FOLLOWS: THE AREA OF A CIRCLE IS πR², AND EACH SUCCESSIVE CIRCLE HAS A RADIUS THAT IS 10% LARGER THAN THE PREVIOUS ONE. THIS MEANS THE RADIUS OF THE NTH CIRCLE IS R × (1.1)^(N-1). THE TOTAL AREA IS THE SUM OF THE AREAS OF ALL 8 CIRCLES. THIS IS A GEOMETRIC SERIES WITH FIRST TERM A = πR² AND COMMON RATIO K = 1.1. THE SUM OF A GEOMETRIC SERIES IS A × (K⁸ - 1)/(K - 1). THE TOTAL AREA IS πR² × (1.1⁸ - 1)/(1.1 - 1). THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS THAT CAN BE PLACED ALONG THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE 5TH CIRCLE IS CALCULATED AS FOLLOWS: THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF A CIRCLE IS 2πR, AND THE NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS THE CIRCUMFERENCE DIVIDED BY THE DISTANCE BETWEEN EACH CHARACTER. THE DISTANCE BETWEEN EACH CHARACTER IS 0.5 CM, SO THE NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS THE CIRCUMFERENCE DIVIDED BY 0.5. THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE 5TH CIRCLE DIVIDED BY 0.5. THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS πR × 2 ÷ 0.5 = 4πR. THE LETTER I APPEARS 11 TIMES IN THE RESPONSE.
